history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed after Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade because Spring Security 6 removed authorizeRequests() in favor of authorizeHttpRequests(), and the migration recipe missed the lambda-style configuration in ActuatorBasicSecurityConfig.java.
history.qwen_rationale: Adding SpringBoot3BestPractices alongside UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 may trigger additional Spring Security 6 migration rules and configuration fixes. Keeping the step under JDK 17 ensures framework compatibility during the transition before bumping to JDK 21.

history.qwen_observation: The build fails during the Spring Boot 3 upgrade step because the git-commit-id-plugin crashes trying to fetch missing git delta objects in the containerized environment.
history.qwen_rationale: Setting the git.skip property to true will bypass the git-commit-id-plugin execution during compilation, allowing the Spring Boot 3 migration recipes to run and compile successfully without being blocked by repository fetch errors.

history.qwen_observation: The spring_boot_3_upgrade step fails during mvn compile because the git-commit-id-plugin attempts to fetch git data and crashes with a TransportException due to a corrupted/shallow git state in the runner environment.
history.qwen_rationale: Adding both git.skip and git.commit.id.skip properties to the POM ensures the git-commit-id-plugin is bypassed during the build phase, allowing mvn compile to succeed so that the Spring Boot 3 and Java 21 migration recipes can execute without being blocked by git state corruption.

history.qwen_observation: The git-commit-id-plugin crashes during recipe execution due to a JGit 'Missing delta base' fetch error, likely caused by a shallow clone or corrupted git state in the CI environment, and previous AddProperty attempts failed to override explicit plugin configuration.
history.qwen_rationale: Switching to ChangePropertyValue ensures the git.skip and git.commit.id.skip properties are forcibly overridden regardless of prior POM values. Upgrading the plugin to 4.9.10 improves JGit compatibility and property handling. This should bypass the fatal fetch error and allow the Spring Boot 3 upgrade to proceed.
